metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5220
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure S1e: STED super-resolution microscopy of hippocampal primary neurons with antibody labeling of endogenous TGN38 (gene TGOLN2, marker of the trans-Golgi network), postsynaptic density marker Homer1 and actin (phalloidin, in confocal mode) showed TGN38 at the dendritic shaft and at postsynapses in close proximity to Homer1.

Authors observed that "While the synaptopodin signal was clearly concentrated in the spine head, calreticulin, ERGIC53 and TGN38 appeared as sparse, but distinct, spots in both the dendritic shaft and spines.". Experimental description: M&M: "Cultures of dissociated rat hippocampal primary neurons were prepared from postnatal P0-P2 Wistar rats ..."

antibody:
TGN38 (Sigma, cat. T9826, 1:100 dilution)

A note on the targeted protein and its orthologs;
The antibody manufacturer links to NCBI Entrez genes 192152 (rat, Tgoln2) and 22134 (mouse, Tgoln1). The RGD database also describes gene TGOLN2 with alias TGN38 (RGD:620445). Further, the HGNC database at genenames.org does not contain a gene TGOLN1, but does have gene TGOLN2 with known alias TGN38.

antibody validation is shown in Figure S11.
